description:
Add continuous point-in-time recovery (PITR) and first-class forking to the SQLite v2 storage layer. Branches become the unit of identity; bookmarks (33-char lex-sortable strings) name commits; `fork()` and `restore_to_bookmark()` are metadata-only at the storage layer (copy-on-write at the layer level). FoundationDB stays the hot tier. S3 (or filesystem in dev) is the cold tier holding LTX V3 layer files for history. A new cold compactor service drains DELTAs into immutable layers, maintains a sparse BookmarkIndex, and runs dependency-graph GC against a 30-day retention window. Both compactors are UPS-driven (no cron). The spec lives at `.agent/specs/sqlite-pitr-fork.md`. Read it first.

===== KEY INVARIANTS =====

- Branches are the unit of identity. An actor's storage = the chain of branches reachable from its head by parent pointers. New keys: `[BRANCHES]/head`, `[BRANCHES]/list/{id}`, `[BR][branch_id]/...` for per-branch storage.
- Bookmarks are 33 chars: `{timestamp_ms_hex_be:016}-{txid_hex_be:016}`. Branch identity is NOT in the wire format; bookmarks are interpreted relative to a branch context. Lex order = chronological within a single branch's parent chain only; siblings break global lex order.
- Fork is metadata-only: regular-read parent's `retention_pin_txid` (OCC fence), depth check (cap MAX_FORK_DEPTH=16), allocate Uuid, write Branch, atomic-min `oldest_descendant_parent_txid`, atomic-add refcount. No data copy. `restore_to_bookmark` is the same primitive; pegboard decides whether to swap head.
- FoundationDB is hot tier. S3 is cold tier (`ColdTier::Disabled` for local dev, `ColdTier::Filesystem` for tests, `ColdTier::S3` for prod, `ColdTier::Faulty` wrapper for fault injection).
- Rolling checksum lives on `/META/head` as `post_apply_checksum: u64` (CRC-ISO-64 XOR fold). Hot-path commit RTT count is unchanged from stateless spec (1).
- `COMMITS/{T}` value is just `i64 LE wall_clock_ms` (no checksum); checksum is on `/META/head` (rolling) and per-layer LTX trailers (cold).
- Cold compactor is UPS-driven (NO periodic cron). Drain trigger fires when hot compactor's pass advances `materialized_txid` past `cold_compact_delta_threshold` (1024 deltas) beyond `cold_drained_txid`. Fork warmup is its own UPS payload. GC self-publishes at end of drain pass.
- Cold pass is Phase A (FDB read tx, <2s) → Phase B (S3-only, no FDB tx) → Phase C (FDB write tx, <3s with OCC fence on `cold_drained_txid`). Phase B uses HWM `pending/{uuid}.marker` files for orphan reconciliation.
- Layer filenames `delta/{min_txid}-{max_txid}.ltx` OMIT checksum so re-uploads are idempotent at the S3 key level.
- GC runs as Phase C-2 holding the cold lease. Pin = min(time_floor_txid, oldest_descendant_parent_txid). NO monotonic ratchet. Frozen branches use creation-relative wall-clock retention, not head-relative.
- Branch deletion cascade: refcount=0 check → state=Tombstoned → clear hot prefix synchronously → cold compactor's next pass walks S3 prefix → state=Deleted. Actor-destroy uses a gasoline workflow for batch DeleteObjects.
- Read path on a fork descendant uses flattened ancestry (ancestors[] vec, capped at MAX_FORK_DEPTH=16, with lazy PIDX cache per ancestor). Worst case 16 ancestors × 2 in-tx gets per pgno on cold cache; mitigated by lazy cache + fork warmup that pre-stages parent image layers in the descendant.
- Burst mode: when cold-pod 5xx ratio exceeds threshold over 3 passes, hot quota cap raises from 10 GiB to 20 GiB (HOT_BURST_MULTIPLIER=2). Reverts when cold tier recovers.
- Billable cold bytes per branch = sum of layer files OWNED by this branch (created by its own cold passes, including fork-warmup-copied layers). Fork descendants start at 0 billable cold bytes.
- All cross-pod-persisted S3 objects (`ColdManifest`, `BranchColdState`, `BookmarkIndex`) carry `schema_version: u32` via vbare. The cold compactor reads old version + writes new version on every pass; reader retains prior-version code paths for one full retention window past any version bump.
- Breaking changes are unconditionally acceptable. The system has not shipped to production. Wire shape, on-disk key layout, and existing schemas are all free to change.

===== ARCHITECTURAL CONTEXT =====

- All work is in `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/`. Spec is `.agent/specs/sqlite-pitr-fork.md`.
- New top-level modules: `pump/branches.rs`, `pump/bookmark.rs`, `pump/checksum.rs`, `pump/ancestry.rs`, `cold_compactor/`, `cold_tier/`, `burst_mode.rs`.
- Cold compactor is a new `ServiceKind::Standalone` service registered in `engine/packages/engine/src/run_config.rs` next to the hot compactor.
- Stateless spec (`sqlite-storage-stateless.md`) is the prerequisite; this work extends it. Hot compactor, ActorDb, META split, lease pattern, UPS triggers, atomic-add quota — all already exist and stay in place.
- All tests live under `tests/` (no inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `src/`).
- Use `vbare::OwnedVersionedData` + versioned `*.bare` schema for all persisted/wire formats. Raw `serde_bare` only for ephemeral in-memory encodings.
- Use `scc::HashMap` / `moka::Cache` / `DashMap` for concurrent maps; never `Mutex<HashMap>`. Use `tokio::sync::Mutex` / `RwLock` in async code; `parking_lot` only in forced-sync contexts.
- Errors: `anyhow` from failable functions, `RivetError` derive for typed errors, no glob imports from anyhow, prefer `.context()` over `anyhow!`.
- Logs: `tracing::info!(?x, "foo")` with structured fields; lowercase messages; no `println!`/`eprintln!`.

===== RUN COMMANDS =====

From repo root:

- Compile-check a crate: `cargo check -p <package-name>` (preferred for verification).
- Build a crate: `cargo build -p <package-name>`.
- Run all tests for a crate: `cargo test -p <package-name>`.
- Run a single test file: `cargo test -p sqlite-storage --test <file_stem>`.
- Run a single test: `cargo test -p <package-name> <test_name> -- --nocapture`.
- Never run `cargo fmt` or `./scripts/cargo/fix.sh` — the team formats at merge time.

===== READ BEFORE STARTING =====

- `.agent/specs/sqlite-pitr-fork.md` — the full v2 spec this PRD implements.
- `.agent/specs/sqlite-storage-stateless.md` — the prerequisite stateless spec.
- `.agent/research/review-architecture.md`, `review-performance.md`, `review-operations.md` — adversarial reviews that shaped v2.
- `engine/CLAUDE.md` — VBARE migration rules, Epoxy keys, SQLite storage tests.
- `CLAUDE.md` at repo root — layer constraints, fail-by-default rules, async lock rules, error handling.
- `docs-internal/engine/sqlite-storage.md` — current SQLite storage crash course.
- `docs-internal/engine/sqlite-vfs.md` — VFS parity rules.
- `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/src/` — current crate (already stateless).
- `engine/packages/sqlite-storage/src/compactor/` — reference pattern for the cold compactor (lease, UPS subscriber, pass orchestration).
